title: Interpret Potential Destination Data For A Parcel On The Sorter
summary: Use this source-based reference procedure to interpret potential destination data for a parcel after the parcel is already on the sorter. The training source explains that the second message includes tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data, and that the destination values represent one or more sortable destinations rather than a ZIP code.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing parcel lifecycle evidence, training material, or message details to explain what potential destination data means for a parcel that is already on the sorter.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to infer ZIP-code resolution logic beyond the source statement that ZIP-to-destination mapping is handled upstream.
Do not use this procedure as a control procedure for changing parcel routing or destination assignments.
Do not use this procedure if the available parcel data does not match the source-described second message context.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret parcel lifecycle message content using the training source and explain that potential destination data represents sortable destination information.

safety_notes: This is an interpretive reference procedure derived from training content, not a physical operating instruction.
Do not infer additional routing, mapping, or destination resolution behavior beyond what the source states.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to parcel lifecycle message details or training evidence
Training slide or transcript describing potential destination meaning

access_required: Access to parcel lifecycle message details or training evidence
system_preconditions: Parcel context is at the lifecycle point where the parcel is already on the sorter and the second message has been triggered.
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1 training segment and associated frame showing the parcel lifecycle second message contents.

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the parcel lifecycle and the second message contents, including potential destination data.

steps.title: Identify the parcel lifecycle point
steps.purpose: Confirm that the interpretation is being made at the correct source-described point in parcel flow.
steps.instruction: Identify that the parcel is already on the sorter and that the second message in the parcel lifecycle has been triggered before interpreting potential destination data.
steps.expected_result: The parcel is placed in the source-described lifecycle context for the second message.
steps.healthy_condition: Available evidence shows the parcel is on the sorter and the second message context applies.
steps.failure_condition: The parcel is not yet at the on-sorter stage, or the message context cannot be tied to the second message described by the source.

actions.label: Confirm lifecycle stage
actions.exact_action: Identify the parcel lifecycle point where the parcel is already on the sorter and the second message has been triggered.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the lifecycle slide section describing the second message after the parcel is on the sorter.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the available parcel evidence does not support that the parcel is at the source-described second-message stage.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: After the second message, the parcel is on the sorter and OptiSweep has dimensional and possible destination information.

steps.title: Verify the second message fields
steps.purpose: Ensure the message data being interpreted matches the field set described by the source.
steps.instruction: Check that the available second-message data includes tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
steps.expected_result: The message data matches the source-described second message field list.
steps.healthy_condition: Tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data are all present in the reviewed evidence.
steps.failure_condition: One or more of the source-described second message fields are missing or the message appears to be a different message type.

actions.label: Review second message contents
actions.exact_action: Check that the available message data includes tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the slide or frame section listing the second message contents: tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data on the sorter, and potential destination data.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed parcel data does not include the source-described second message fields.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide OCR: second message includes potential destination data.
The slide describes a second Chat message containing tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data on the sorter, and potential destination data.
The second message contains tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data on the sorter, and potential destination data.

steps.title: Determine whether one or multiple destinations are shown
steps.purpose: Recognize that the source allows for either a single destination or multiple potential destinations for one parcel.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the parcel is described as having one potential destination or multiple potential destinations.
steps.expected_result: The reviewed parcel evidence is categorized as showing either a single sortable destination or multiple sortable destinations.
steps.healthy_condition: The destination data can be described as one or more potential destinations without adding unsupported assumptions.
steps.failure_condition: The destination data cannot be determined from the available evidence or requires unsupported interpretation.

actions.label: Observe destination count
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the parcel is described as having one or multiple potential destinations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the training explanation that UPS may give an order multiple destinations.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop interpretation if destination count cannot be determined from the available source-aligned evidence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: UPS gives this order multiple destinations; it is not a ZIP code, it is a sorted destination.
Potential destination refers to one or more sortable destinations associated with a parcel.

steps.title: Interpret destination values as sortable destinations
steps.purpose: Apply the source definition of potential destination correctly.
steps.instruction: Interpret the listed destination values as sortable destinations, not as a ZIP code.
steps.expected_result: The destination data is explained as sorter-relevant destination information rather than postal ZIP information.
steps.healthy_condition: The explanation clearly states that the values are sorted or sortable destinations.
steps.failure_condition: The values are incorrectly described as ZIP codes or as unsupported routing logic.

actions.label: Apply destination meaning
actions.exact_action: Interpret the listed destination values as sortable destinations, not as a ZIP code.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the explanation that destination is not a ZIP code and instead represents sorted destination information.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if stakeholders require ZIP-code interpretation not supported by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: UPS gives this order multiple destinations; it is not a ZIP code, it is a sorted destination.
Potential destination refers to one or more sortable destinations associated with a parcel, not the parcel's ZIP code.

steps.title: Note that ZIP-to-destination mapping is upstream
steps.purpose: Set the correct boundary for what the local system knows versus what is handled upstream.
steps.instruction: If ZIP code mapping is being discussed, note that the source says ZIP-to-destination mapping is handled upstream and the local system only knows the destination information it needs for sorting.
steps.expected_result: The explanation distinguishes upstream ZIP mapping from local destination usage.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation stays within the source boundary that upstream systems perform ZIP-to-destination mapping.
steps.failure_condition: The explanation attributes ZIP mapping logic to the local system without source support.

actions.label: State upstream mapping boundary
actions.exact_action: If ZIP code mapping is being discussed, note that the source says ZIP-to-destination mapping is handled upstream and the local system only knows the destination information it needs for sorting.

stop_or_escalate_if: Do not infer destination resolution logic beyond the source statement that mapping is handled upstream.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: ZIP code to destination mapping is described as an upstream UPS function using a mapping file, with mappings that may be single or multiple and dynamic. The local system only knows destination values.
Transcript: a ZIP code can be mapped to a single destination or multiple destinations; this comes from the ASX system; the local system only knows where it needs to go.

steps.title: Record why multiple potential destinations can exist
steps.purpose: Capture the source-supported explanation for multiple potential destinations on a single parcel.
steps.instruction: Record that multiple potential destinations can exist for a single parcel because upstream mapping may associate one ZIP code with one or more sortable destinations.
steps.expected_result: The final explanation includes the source-supported reason that multiple sortable destinations may be associated with one parcel.
steps.healthy_condition: The explanation states that upstream mapping can produce one or multiple sortable destinations for a parcel.
steps.failure_condition: The explanation adds unsupported rules, thresholds, or decision logic beyond the source.

actions.label: Document multiple-destination explanation
actions.exact_action: Record that multiple potential destinations can exist for a single parcel because upstream mapping may associate one ZIP code with one or more sortable destinations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the training explanation that one ZIP code may map to one or multiple sortable destinations.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a more detailed mapping rule or resolution algorithm is requested, because the source does not provide it.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: UPS gives this order multiple destinations; it is not a ZIP code, it is a sorted destination.
Transcript: a ZIP code can be mapped to a single destination or multiple destinations; this comes from the ASX system; the local system only knows where it needs to go.
A ZIP code can be mapped to a single destination or multiple destinations upstream.

success_criteria: The user can correctly explain that potential destination data represents one or more sortable destinations for the parcel on the sorter.
The user distinguishes sortable destination information from ZIP code information.
The explanation stays within the source boundary that ZIP-to-destination mapping is handled upstream.
healthy_conditions: The parcel is interpreted in the correct lifecycle context: already on the sorter after the second message.
The reviewed message data includes tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
Potential destination values are described as one or more sortable destinations, not ZIP codes.
failure_conditions: The available parcel data does not include the source-described second message fields.
The parcel is interpreted outside the source-described lifecycle point.
Destination values are treated as ZIP codes rather than sortable destinations.
The explanation invents local destination resolution logic not supported by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed parcel data does not include the source-described second message fields.
Escalate if a detailed ZIP-to-destination resolution rule is required, because the source only states that mapping is handled upstream.
Escalate for SME review if stakeholders need operational or system-specific behavior beyond this training explanation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Parcel lifecycle slide showing the second message contents and the discussion of potential destination meaning.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript: UPS gives this order multiple destinations; it is not a ZIP code, it is a sorted destination.
Slide OCR: second message includes potential destination data.

missing_details: The source does not provide a named OptiSweep screen or exact UI workflow for viewing the second message fields.
The source does not provide commands, APIs, or database queries for retrieving parcel message data.
The source does not define escalation ownership beyond the need for further review when source-aligned evidence is missing or more detailed mapping logic is requested.
The source is training content and does not provide a formal validation method beyond interpretive consistency with the transcript and slide.
